title: Copy and Paste Screenshots Directly into monday.com
excerpt: I would like to suggest the ability to copy and paste images directly into monday.com, particularly screenshots. When working on bugs, I often need to take screenshots and quickly paste them into Jira. This feature saves…

posts.author: Mario.Osborn.Dechert (Mario)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to suggest the ability to copy and paste images directly into monday.com, particularly screenshots. When working on bugs, I often need to take screenshots and quickly paste them into Jira. This feature saves a significant amount of time. However, in monday.com, I currently have to take a screenshot, save it to my desktop, upload the image to a monday.com card, and then delete the image from my desktop. This process is cumbersome and time-consuming.

Additionally, it would be highly beneficial if these images could be placed inline with the text. Often, images need to be integrated with text to form a cohesive request or explanation of work. Having this capability would greatly enhance the efficiency and usability of monday.com for bug tracking and other tasks.

Thank you for considering this feature request.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Mario, this is a great feature request - pasting screenshots directly into monday.com would save so much time!

If you’re looking for a workaround that’s already available, check out Capture for monday.com. It’s an app + Chrome extension that brings screenshot functionality directly into your workflow. With it, you can:

  • Capture screenshots right from your browser

  • Annotate and highlight UI elements

  • Instantly convert your feedback into monday.com tasks

In practice, rather than needing to save a screenshot, upload it, then delete it, and hope it appears inline you can capture it, annotate it, and send it straight to your task. It won’t fully replicate inline paste into update text, but it’s a massively faster and more seamless alternative.

Where to get it:

⚠ To use Capture, you must have two extensions installed:
  • monday.com account with the Capture for monday.com app installed.
  • Capture in Chrome for monday.com and Jira extension from the Chrome Web Store.

Instead of saving, uploading, and deleting screenshots later, you can capture, annotate, and send them straight to the right task.

There’s a 14-day free trial, so you can test it with your team before deciding.

You can find more info on our web-site, or type your question below and I’ll help you
